free_text: Yields and Recoil Properties of Scandium Isotopes
Produced in the Interaction of 590 MeV and 18 GeV
The targets for both the yield consisted of a stack of
carefully aligned, 99.9% pure metal foils-(30-mu-m Al)+
+(10-mu-m Al)+(30-mu-m Al). The Uranium foil was
cleaned to remove any oxide layer.
. F/B ratios of reaction products were measured.
/by Authors/.The Absolute yields were determined by
normalizing the relative isotopic yields to the cross
section of Sc-48 measured in separate experiments
without isotope separation. The 1.31 MeV gamma-ray of
Sc-48 was measured with a calibrated Ge(Li) detector.

Possible secondary effects on the monitor reaction were
checked in some on the 18 GeV experiments by also
counting 22Na from the reaction 27Al(p,3p3n)22Na in the
monitor foils, using a calibrated 3 x 3 in. NaI
detector. Thus secondary reactions increasing the 24Na
